mission.title: kubeflow: API calls on centraldashboard become slow with a large number of profiles
mission.description: API calls on centraldashboard become slow with a large number of profiles. This issue affects 5+ users.

steps.title: Identify kubeflow troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kubeflow deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=kubeflow
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: API calls on centraldashboard become slow with a large number of profiles

steps.title: Check current Service configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubeflow resources:
```bash
kubectl get service -A
kubectl describe service <name> -n <namespace>
```

steps.title: Apply the fix for API calls on centraldashboard become slow with a large numbe
steps.description:
To solve https://github.com/kubeflow/kubeflow/issues/5190

Found most of the issue to occur when listing the rolebindings when there are many namespaces as it does a loop. Query the shared index to list and get the rolebindings.

* Note: first commit and if there are procedures I am missing, please 
```yaml
GET /kfam/v1/bindings?user=$USER ReadBinding 6.064392368s
```

steps.title: Confirm API calls on centraldashboard become slow with a l is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: To solve https://github.com/kubeflow/kubeflow/issues/5190

Found most of the issue to occur when listing the rolebindings when there are many namespaces as it does a loop. Query the shared index to list and get the rolebindings.

* Note: first commit and if there are procedures I am missing, please let me know.. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubeflow installed or the issue environment reproducible.
